value.oil_futures: closed for weekend; electronic reopen Sunday ~18:00 ET
value.june_19_status: Juneteenth - U.S. fixed income and equity cash closed

text: June 20, 2026 is a Saturday with no U.S. Treasury, FX, equity or futures session, so this macro cycle is an explicit carry-forward: the Treasury curve carries its June 18 last print (June 19 was Juneteenth), while the dollar and Brent carry their Friday, June 19 closes; oil and equity-index futures are shut until the Sunday electronic reopen around 18:00 ET, leaving geopolitical headline risk around the Strait of Hormuz as the only live weekend macro channel.

calculation.method: read the official Treasury par-yield CSV live; 10Y minus 2Y for the last available print; confirmed no 06/19/2026 or 06/20/2026 row exists

text: With no June 19 (Juneteenth) or June 20 (weekend) print, the official Treasury par-yield CSV read live on June 20 still ends at June 18, 2026 with the 2-year at 4.19%, the 10-year at 4.46% and the 30-year at 4.90% - a +27 bp 2s10s spread that had flattened from +29 bp on June 17 - so U.S. rates were carried unchanged at Thursday's close through the long weekend.

value.high_basis: near one-year / 13-month highs; fresh 52-week intraday high 101.13 dated 06/19/26
value.fed_policy_context: hawkish Fed; about half of FOMC project at least one 2026 hike; Chair Kevin Warsh stresses above-target inflation; markets price ~50% odds of a 25 bp September hike
value.session_note: last live FX print Friday 2026-06-19; FX closed Saturday

calculation.method: vendor DXY close; Trading Economics spot cross-checked against the CNBC .DXY quote prior close and 52-week-high stamp

text: The U.S. Dollar Index carried Friday's strength into the June 20 weekend at around 100.8 (Trading Economics spot 100.7852, about -0.06% on June 19; CNBC prior close 100.849), holding near a one-year / 13-month high after a fresh 52-week intraday high of 101.13 dated 06/19/26 and up roughly 1.1% on the week; the move is policy-driven - a hawkish Fed with about half of FOMC projecting a 2026 hike, new Chair Kevin Warsh, and ~50% market odds of a 25 bp September hike - and is a carried level with FX closed Saturday.

value.weekend_status: oil futures closed Saturday; electronic reopen Sunday ~18:00 ET
value.gap_risk: Iran's June 20 Hormuz re-closure declaration is an unpriced weekend headline, skewing the reopen higher if it gains traction
value.us_counterclaim: U.S./CENTCOM say the strait remains open; 55 ships and 17M+ bbl transited Saturday
value.friday_flow_note: thin Friday Gulf traffic; Tehran demanded mandatory vessel insurance

calculation.method: Brent front-month last print from Trading Economics; weekend gap-risk read qualitatively from dated June 20 Hormuz headlines

text: Brent's last print was $80.59/bbl on June 19, up 0.93% on the day but down roughly 8% on the week as the week unwound the Middle East risk premium; with crude futures closed Saturday and reopening only Sunday evening ET, that $80.59 is the standing reference and Iran's June 20 Hormuz re-closure declaration is an unpriced weekend gap risk - skewed higher if the closure claim gains traction but capped by the U.S. assertion that 55 ships and 17M+ barrels still transited Saturday.

value.iran_declaration: Iran's joint military command and IRGC declared the strait closed again on Saturday 2026-06-20 and warned vessels away
value.iran_reason: continued Israeli operations in Lebanon plus alleged U.S. bad faith / breach of the interim truce
value.us_position: strait remains open; 'Iran does not control the Strait of Hormuz' - CENTCOM Capt. Tim Hawkins
value.us_flow_claim: 55 merchant ships and more than 17 million barrels of oil transited Saturday
value.lebanon: Israeli strikes killed at least 16 on Saturday; war death toll passed 4,000; Israeli military received a cease-fire directive; Hezbollah says Iran will not reopen the strait until Israel publicly commits to a comprehensive ceasefire
value.interim_deal: memorandum of understanding signed Wednesday 2026-06-17 after ~4 months of war; required an end to Israeli action in Lebanon and a toll-free Hormuz reopening for at least 60 days

text: On Saturday, June 20, 2026 Iran's joint military command and the IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again and warned vessels away, citing continued Israeli operations in Lebanon and alleged U.S. bad faith on the interim truce; the U.S. denied it, with CENTCOM's Capt. Tim Hawkins saying 'Iran does not control the Strait of Hormuz' and the military reporting 55 ships and 17M+ barrels transiting Saturday, while Israeli strikes in Lebanon killed at least 16 and a Hezbollah official said Iran will not reopen the strait until Israel publicly commits to a comprehensive ceasefire - making the chokepoint a real headline risk but a contested physical disruption.

value.start: technical-level talks due to begin Sunday 2026-06-21
value.us_team: Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff already in Switzerland working technical details
value.iran_team: delegation traveling to join (Speaker Qalibaf, FM Araghchi, plus central-bank and oil officials)
value.window: 60 days to convert the interim MoU into a final nuclear deal (extendable)
value.trump_toll_threat: Truth Social post pledged no Hormuz tolls during the 60-day ceasefire but threatened U.S. tolls afterward for 'services rendered' if no final deal is reached
value.objectives: secure Iran's enriched-uranium stockpile; unfreeze Iranian assets

text: Technical-level U.S.-Iran talks were due to begin Sunday, June 21, 2026 in Switzerland, where Jared Kushner and Steve Witkoff were already working the details and an Iranian delegation (Speaker Qalibaf, FM Araghchi and central-bank and oil officials) was traveling to join, with a 60-day window to turn Wednesday's memorandum of understanding into a final nuclear deal; President Trump kept leverage in play via a Saturday Truth Social post pledging no Hormuz tolls during the ceasefire but threatening U.S. tolls afterward for 'services rendered' absent a final deal.
